loyaltyMetaDescription: Member loyalty overview, discovery, and business detail surfaces.
memberAuthRequiredRouteSummaryMessage: This route belongs to the authenticated member portal. Sign in or create an account first, then continue to the protected destination instead of falling back to a minimal access block.
memberAuthRequiredFollowUpMessage: Protected entry points should still stay connected to the wider front-office system, so you can continue through storefront and account surfaces before returning to the authenticated route.
memberAuthRequiredOfferBoardMessage: Even protected entry walls should keep several strong buying options visible so the shopper does not lose commercial momentum while deciding how to sign in.

memberAuthRequiredOfferBoardEmptyMessage: No live buying opportunities are currently visible from this protected entry wall.
accountHubTitle: Public self-service flows are now split from the future member session.
accountHubDescription: Registration, activation, and password recovery can move forward now. Full profile, addresses, and authenticated member navigation remain intentionally blocked on the final browser auth/session decision.
accountHubReadinessMessage: Cart status: {cartStatus}. Cart lines: {cartLineCount}. CMS status: {cmsStatus}. Category status: {categoriesStatus}. Public account entry should keep the current storefront state visible before the shopper crosses into sign-in or registration.

accountHubActionCenterMessage: Public account entry should still help the shopper move through live commerce and content surfaces instead of stopping at auth-only cards.
accountHubActionCartDescription: {itemCount} active lines totaling {total} are ready for another checkout attempt.
accountHubActionCartFallbackDescription: No active cart is visible right now, so the next best move is to sign in and reopen the intended storefront route.

accountHubActionCatalogFallbackDescripti: Continue storefront discovery from the public catalog even before the auth step completes.
accountHubActionProductDescription: This is currently the strongest visible buying opportunity from the public catalog, available for {price}.
accountHubActionProductFallbackDescripti: No live product opportunity is currently visible, so the next best move stays on account recovery and catalog browsing.

accountHubRouteMapTitle: Move across public account entry routes
accountHubRouteMapDescription: Account self-service should stay connected to the wider storefront system, so public account entry does not become an isolated auth island.
accountHubHomeDescription: Go back to the composed storefront entry surface to review the broader CMS, catalog, and member system state.
accountHubCatalogDescription: Carry public account entry back into browse and product discovery without leaving the shared storefront shell.
accountHubCmsDescription: Move from self-service entry into public CMS pages so content, commerce, and account entry stay visible as one front-office system.
accountHubCompositionJourneyMessage: Public account entry should also behave like a route-level journey that keeps the current entry task, the next recovery or checkout move, and storefront continuation visible instead of acting like an isolated auth island.
accountHubCompositionJourneyCurrentTitle: Keep public account entry in view
accountHubCompositionJourneyCurrentDescr: This route should keep the preferred post-auth path of {returnPath} explicit while the shopper decides between sign-in, registration, activation, and recovery.

accountHubCompositionJourneyStorefrontDe: The strongest visible public storefront opportunity is currently available for {price}.
accountHubCompositionJourneyStorefrontFa: A live storefront path remains available when the shopper wants to widen the current account-entry journey with content or browse context.
accountHubCompositionRouteMapMessage: Public account entry should hand off cleanly into cart review, auth recovery, content context, and catalog browse instead of stopping at a cluster of auth cards.
accountHubCompositionRouteMapCurrentTitl: Keep the current account route visible

accountHubCompositionRouteMapNextCartDes: {itemCount} visible cart lines totaling {total} can still anchor the next storefront move before auth recovery continues.
accountHubCompositionRouteMapNextAuthDes: Sign-in remains the main account recovery path when no active basket currently needs immediate follow-up.

publicAuthReturnOrdersDescription: This auth flow was entered from a protected order route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the relevant order surface instead of restarting from the dashboard.
publicAuthReturnInvoicesDescription: This auth flow was entered from a protected invoice route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the relevant invoice surface instead of restarting from the dashboard.
publicAuthReturnLoyaltyDescription: This auth flow was entered from a loyalty route. After auth succeeds, the member should return to the loyalty surface that triggered the sign-in wall.

publicAuthCompositionJourneyRegisterDesc: Registration should stay connected to the preferred post-auth path at {returnPath} instead of behaving like a detached account-creation form.

publicAuthCompositionJourneyActivationNe: Activation should flow directly into sign-in once the shopper is ready to open the protected account route.

publicAuthCompositionRouteMapSignInDescr: Protected account access should stay close to the rest of public auth instead of becoming a detached terminal step.
publicAuthCompositionRouteMapRegisterDes: Registration should stay one step away from sign-in and recovery so account entry remains coherent.
publicAuthCompositionRouteMapActivationD: Activation follow-up should stay one step away from registration and sign-in while account setup is still in progress.

publicAuthCompositionRouteMapPasswordDes: Password recovery should stay one step away from sign-in so protected-route access can continue cleanly after reset.
publicAuthCompositionRouteMapPasswordNex: Once recovery is done, sign-in should remain the next stable handoff into the protected route.

sessionStrategyNoteDescription: Login, profile editing, address book, orders, invoices, and loyalty remain behind the browser-session decision. This keeps Darwin.Web from hard-coding a token transport that the wider platform has not yet finalized.
cardSignInTitle: Sign in to the protected portal
cardSignInDescription: Use the provisional browser session layer to open profile, orders, invoices, and loyalty pages in the web portal.
cardRegisterDescription: Consumer self-service registration is now available directly against the member auth contract.
cardActivationDescription: The current backend activation lifecycle is now exposed in the storefront instead of staying implicit.
cardPasswordTitle: Request or complete a password reset
cardPasswordDescription: Password reset now stays on the public web route structure while still using the existing backend email/token flow.
registerDescription: This screen registers a member directly through the public member auth endpoint and then relies on the existing backend confirmation-email lifecycle.
registrationSubmittedMessage: The account was created. Check the email confirmation flow before expecting sign-in to succeed.
currentBoundaryRegistration: Registration is live, but browser sign-in persistence is still a separate decision.
currentBoundaryCommunication: Confirmation email behavior stays on the backend Communication Core path.
activationDescription: This route exposes the existing backend confirmation lifecycle to the storefront without inventing a web-only activation system.
activationRequestedMessage: If the account exists, the activation email has been requested through the current backend flow.
activationConfirmedMessage: The email confirmation request completed successfully. Browser sign-in is still a separate session decision.
activationRecoveryDescription: Public auth routes should not leave activation recovery hidden behind a separate route. Request another confirmation email here or continue into the dedicated activation flow with the same return path.
activationEmailRequiredMessage: Email is required to request confirmation.
activationRequestFailedMessage: Activation email could not be requested.
activationEmailTokenRequiredMessage: Email and token are both required to confirm the account.
activationConfirmFailedMessage: Email confirmation could not be completed.
passwordRecoveryTitle: Request or complete a password reset
passwordRecoveryDescription: This route exposes the existing backend reset-token lifecycle and keeps the web client out of its own private reset implementation.
passwordRequestedMessage: If the account exists, the password-reset email has been requested through the current backend flow.
passwordResetMessage: The password reset completed successfully. Browser sign-in remains a separate session decision.
passwordRequestEmailRequiredMessage: Email is required to request a password reset.
passwordRequestFailedMessage: Password reset request could not be completed.
passwordResetFieldsRequiredMessage: Email, token, and new password are required.
signInDescription: This first browser-auth slice uses web-owned cookies to hold the member session while keeping all protected data behind the canonical member endpoints.
memberAuthHttpErrorMessage: The member auth request could not be completed.
memberAuthNetworkErrorMessage: Member auth API could not be reached.
memberSessionUnauthorizedMessage: The member session is no longer authorized.
memberResourceNotFoundMessage: The requested member resource was not found.
memberApiHttpErrorMessage: The member API request could not be completed.
memberApiInvalidPayloadMessage: The member API returned an invalid response payload.
memberApiNetworkErrorMessage: The member API could not be reached.
sessionNoteArchitecture: This is a provisional browser-session layer owned by Darwin.Web, not a permanent auth architecture decision.
sessionNoteApi: Orders, invoices, loyalty, and profile data still come only from Darwin.WebApi.
memberDataWarningsTitle: Some member data loaded with warnings.
memberDashboardDescription: This dashboard reads the authenticated member profile surfaces through the new web-owned browser session layer.
memberRouteSummaryMessage: Profile status: {profileStatus}. Preferences status: {preferencesStatus}. CRM context status: {customerContextStatus}.
profileUnavailable: Profile data is not currently available for this member session.
preferencesUnavailable: Preference data is not currently available for this member session.
portalRoutesDescription: Use the same member-portal navigation across dashboard, profile, preferences, addresses, orders, invoices, and loyalty so these surfaces behave like one system.
ordersPortalNote: Order history now sits inside the same member-portal navigation as profile, preferences, addresses, invoices, and loyalty so post-purchase follow-up is not a detached route.
invoicesPortalNote: Invoice history now shares the same member-portal navigation and should behave like part of the same billing and account surface instead of a separate archive page.
orderDetailPortalNote: Order detail stays inside the shared member-portal navigation so payment retry, shipments, invoices, and account follow-up remain connected.
invoiceDetailPortalNote: Invoice detail stays inside the shared member-portal navigation so billing follow-up, linked orders, and account context remain connected.

memberCrossSurfaceLoyaltyDescription: Continue into discovery, rewards, and business follow-up without treating loyalty like a detached side journey.

securityConfirmMismatchMessage: The new password and confirmation must match.
